chunk_map.heading: H1 — Learn from the test, re-send packaged

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Referent resolution under a sequential deck read.
rules_evaluated.evidence: chunk 8 "Bring to Module 6": line 124: "Read the scrollback or commits from the Debrief re-send" — "Debrief" never appears anywhere in this file's student-facing body (chunks 0-8) before this point; it's only glossed in the post-maintainer-cut trainer material (session-runtime breakdown). A cold sequential read of this file has no antecedent for it.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Replace with a self-resolving phrase, e.g. "the re-send you just did," or name the beat earlier in the body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every backward reference points at a beat that exists.
Live-demo beats survive a text read.

rules_evaluated.evidence: chunk 6 "Key Concepts": lines 60-66 are genuine key-concept bullets, but the same chunk (no `##` break) runs on through line 116 with the full M5 long-run re-send session — a new-session prompt, `/rename`, `/clear`, the walk-away re-send prompt (lines 94-108), and RLHF/candid-report guidance. "Key Concepts" doesn't literal-truth a slide that spends most of its length on live execution steps.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Split at line 68 ("Now the re-send.") under a new heading (e.g. "Re-send the packaged task") so "Key Concepts" covers only the bullets.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Internal dialect never lands on a slide.
